seoTitle: Add a Filter in a Confluence Table (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to add a filter to a Confluence table in 2026, from the header funnel icon on published pages to advanced multi-rule Table Filter macros.
intro: An unfiltered data grid forces readers to scroll through outdated metrics to find what matters. This guide shows how to add filters to a Confluence table in 2026, from a quick header click to advanced multi-column rules.

steps.heading: Verify the table is published
steps.body: Open your target page and confirm your main data grid layout is fully published.

steps.heading: Use the header filter icon
steps.body: Hover over the table header and click the native funnel or filter icon.

steps.heading: Search a column
steps.body: Select your target column and type a search string to instantly hide mismatched rows.

steps.heading: Use the advanced filter macro
steps.body: In edit mode, use `/table filter` to drop an interactive control box for more complex grids.

steps.heading: Layer your rules
steps.body: Toggle checkboxes, text matching rules, and value ranges, layering multiple column rules together.

steps.heading: Publish
steps.body: Click Publish to lock down the interactive filtered layout.

faq.question: Where do I find the filter option on a Confluence table?
faq.answer: Hover over the table header on a published page and click the native funnel or filter icon that appears.

faq.question: Can I filter by multiple conditions at once?
faq.answer: Yes, layering multiple column rules simultaneously lets you isolate high-priority rows that match several conditions.

faq.question: Does filtering change the underlying table data?
faq.answer: No, filtering only isolates rows for viewing and doesn't modify or drop the underlying data structure.

faq.question: Can I nest a filtered table inside another data macro?
faq.answer: Yes, you can nest tables inside dedicated Table Filter and Charts panels for deeper reporting.
